subtasks.title: Create feed screen with FlatList and vertical paging
subtasks.description: Set up the main feed screen at app/(tabs)/index.tsx as the home tab with a vertically scrolling FlatList using pagingEnabled for full-screen snap behavior.
subtasks.details: Create app/(tabs)/index.tsx as the home tab screen. Implement a FlatList with pagingEnabled=true, showsVerticalScrollIndicator=false, and decelerationRate='fast' for TikTok-style snapping. Each item should occupy full screen height using useWindowDimensions(). Set up proper TypeScript types for the feed item data structure matching the properties + property_videos schema (id, price, address, bedrooms, bathrooms, owner_user_id, agency_id, playback_url, thumbnail_url). Use keyExtractor with property.id. Add getItemLayout for performance optimization since all items have fixed height. Configure initialNumToRender=2 and maxToRenderPerBatch=3 for memory efficiency.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that feed screen loads as home tab, FlatList renders with vertical scrolling, and items snap to full screen boundaries on swipe.

subtasks.title: Implement full-screen video player with expo-video
subtasks.description: Create a VideoFeedItem component using expo-video's VideoView for each feed item, displaying property videos at full screen with proper aspect ratio handling.
subtasks.details: Create components/feed/VideoFeedItem.tsx using expo-video (not expo-av). Import { VideoView, useVideoPlayer } from 'expo-video'. Each video player should fill the screen with resizeMode='cover' for vertical videos. Accept props: playbackUrl, thumbnailUrl, isActive (controls playback), onVideoEnd. Initialize the player with useVideoPlayer(playbackUrl, player => { player.loop = true }). Set player.play() or player.pause() based on isActive prop using useEffect. Add poster image using thumbnail_url while video loads. Handle errors gracefully with fallback UI. Style container with position: 'absolute', backgroundColor: '#000' for immersive dark feed aesthetic per PRD.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that videos render at full screen, play when isActive=true, pause when isActive=false, loop correctly, and display thumbnails while loading.

subtasks.title: Implement viewability detection for autoplay/pause
subtasks.description: Add viewability logic to autoplay videos when 70% visible and pause when scrolled away, tracking the currently active video index.
subtasks.details: Implement viewabilityConfig on FlatList: { itemVisiblePercentThreshold: 70, minimumViewTime: 100 }. Use onViewableItemsChanged callback with useCallback to track which item is most visible. Store activeIndex in useState. Pass isActive={index === activeIndex} prop to each VideoFeedItem. Use viewabilityConfigCallbackPairs ref pattern to avoid FlatList remounting issues. Handle edge cases: pause all when app goes to background using AppState listener from react-native. Resume playback when returning to foreground. Add useFocusEffect from @react-navigation/native to pause when navigating away from feed tab.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that videos autoplay when scrolled into view at 70%+ visibility, pause when scrolled away, pause on app background, and resume on foreground.

subtasks.title: Implement video preloading for smooth scrolling
subtasks.description: Preload the next 2 videos ahead of the current position to ensure smooth scrolling without loading delays.
subtasks.details: Create a preloading strategy using expo-video's prefetch capabilities. Maintain a preloadedVideos ref tracking which URLs have been preloaded. When activeIndex changes, trigger preload for indices activeIndex+1 and activeIndex+2. Use expo-video's Asset.loadAsync or equivalent prefetch method if available, otherwise implement by creating hidden VideoPlayer instances that load but don't render. Implement windowSize={5} on FlatList to keep 5 items mounted (2 before, current, 2 after). Add removeClippedSubviews={true} for Android performance. Track preload state per video to show loading indicator if video at current index isn't ready yet.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that scrolling to next/previous videos shows no loading delay, preloading occurs for upcoming videos, and memory stays bounded.

subtasks.title: Create Supabase query for active properties with videos
subtasks.description: Implement the data fetching layer to query active properties with ready videos, including cursor-based pagination with limit=10.
subtasks.details: Create lib/queries/feedProperties.ts with async function fetchFeedProperties(cursor?: string). Query: select id, price, address, bedrooms, bathrooms, owner_user_id, agency_id, created_at, property_videos(id, playback_url, thumbnail_url, position) from properties where status='active' and deleted_at is null and exists(select 1 from property_videos pv where pv.property_id = properties.id and pv.status='ready' and pv.deleted_at is null) order by created_at desc limit 10. If cursor provided, add filter: created_at < cursor. Return { data: Property[], nextCursor: string | null }. Use Supabase nested select syntax per lineamientos §5.13. Add TypeScript types for FeedProperty matching the query shape. Handle Supabase errors and return typed response.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test query returns active properties with ready videos, pagination works with cursor, and returns empty array gracefully when no more items.

subtasks.title: Build overlay UI with property info card and action buttons
subtasks.description: Create the overlay UI showing property information (price, address, bedrooms/bathrooms) at the bottom and floating like/save buttons.
subtasks.details: Create components/feed/PropertyOverlay.tsx with props: property (price, address, bedrooms, bathrooms, owner), onLike, onSave, isLiked, isSaved, onAgentPress. Bottom card: semi-transparent dark gradient background (LinearGradient from expo-linear-gradient), display formatted price (Intl.NumberFormat for MXN currency), address, bed/bath icons with counts. Right side floating buttons: heart icon for like (outline/filled based on isLiked), bookmark icon for save (outline/filled based on isSaved). Agent avatar in bottom-right corner, tappable to view profile. Use absolute positioning over the video. Apply safe area insets for notch/home indicator. Style per PRD: feed is dark and immersive, use white text with shadow for readability.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test overlay displays property info correctly, buttons show correct state, taps trigger callbacks, and UI is readable over various video content.

subtasks.title: Implement double-tap to like gesture
subtasks.description: Add double-tap gesture recognition on the video to trigger like action with visual heart animation feedback.
subtasks.details: Wrap VideoFeedItem in react-native-gesture-handler's TapGestureHandler with numberOfTaps={2}. On double-tap: trigger onLike callback, show animated heart icon in center of screen using react-native-reanimated (fade in, scale up, then fade out after 800ms). Track last tap timestamp to differentiate from single taps. Ensure gesture doesn't conflict with scroll gestures. Add haptic feedback using expo-haptics (Haptics.impactAsync(ImpactFeedbackStyle.Light)). If already liked, double-tap should still show animation but not unlike (matches TikTok behavior). Create HeartAnimation component with Animated.View and opacity/scale animations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test double-tap triggers like callback, shows heart animation, provides haptic feedback, and doesn't interfere with scrolling.

subtasks.title: Add swipe navigation between videos
subtasks.description: Ensure swipe up advances to next video and swipe down returns to previous, with smooth snap behavior.
subtasks.details: FlatList with pagingEnabled already handles basic swipe-to-snap. Fine-tune with snapToAlignment='start' and snapToInterval={screenHeight}. Add velocity threshold detection: if swipe velocity > threshold, scroll to next/previous item even if drag distance is small. Use onScrollEndDrag and onMomentumScrollEnd to detect final position and update activeIndex. Implement scrollToIndex for programmatic navigation if needed. Add bounces={false} or limited bounce on edges. Ensure smooth 60fps animation by optimizing render cycle - use React.memo on VideoFeedItem, avoid re-renders during scroll using useCallback for all handlers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test swipe up advances to next video, swipe down returns to previous, snapping is smooth at 60fps, and edge bouncing is minimal.

subtasks.title: Implement loading skeleton, pull-to-refresh, and memory management
subtasks.description: Add loading skeleton while initial data loads, pull-to-refresh for new properties, and proper memory management to prevent leaks with many videos.
subtasks.details: Create components/feed/FeedSkeleton.tsx: full-screen dark placeholder with shimmer animation (using react-native-skeleton-placeholder or custom LinearGradient animation) showing ghost UI for video area and property card. Show skeleton on initial load (isFetching && !data). Implement pull-to-refresh with RefreshControl on FlatList, onRefresh resets cursor and refetches. For memory management: implement cleanup in VideoFeedItem useEffect return - pause and unload video player. Use FlatList's removeClippedSubviews={true}. Limit windowSize to prevent too many videos in memory. Monitor with expo-dev-client's performance tools. Add onEndReached for infinite scroll pagination (fetch next page when within 3 items of end). Track loading/error states with useState.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test skeleton shows during initial load, pull-to-refresh fetches new data, infinite scroll loads more, and memory doesn't grow unbounded when scrolling through 50+ videos.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down video feed into: (1) create feed screen with FlatList and vertical paging, (2) implement full-screen video player with expo-video, (3) implement viewability detection for autoplay/pause, (4) implement video preloading for smooth scrolling, (5) create Supabase query for active properties with videos, (6) build overlay UI with property info and action buttons, (7) implement double-tap to like gesture, (8) add swipe navigation and smooth animations, (9) implement loading skeleton, pull-to-refresh, pagination, and memory management

subtasks.title: Fetch active properties and convert PostGIS geography to lat/lng
subtasks.description: Query active properties with location data from Supabase and transform PostGIS geography(Point, 4326) to latitude/longitude coordinates for markers.
subtasks.details: Create hook useMapProperties() in lib/hooks/ that fetches properties with: SELECT id, price, location, operation_type, property_type, bedrooms, bathrooms, address FROM properties WHERE status='active' AND deleted_at IS NULL. PostGIS geography is stored as Point(4326) - use ST_Y(location::geometry) for latitude and ST_X(location::geometry) for longitude in the query, or extract from GeoJSON if Supabase returns it that way. Define TypeScript interface MapProperty with id, price, lat, lng, operationType, propertyType, bedrooms, bathrooms, address. Handle loading/error states. Cache results appropriately.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify query returns correct properties with coordinates. Test with mock data to ensure PostGIS conversion is accurate. Confirm lat/lng values fall within expected Guadalajara region bounds.

subtasks.title: Implement clustering with react-native-maps-clustering

subtasks.details: Install react-native-maps-clustering with pnpm add react-native-maps-clustering. Replace standard MapView with ClusteredMapView wrapper. Configure clustering options: radius=50 (pixels), maxZoom=16, minPoints=2 (minimum markers to form cluster). Style cluster markers to show count badge with property count. Implement onClusterPress to zoom into cluster bounds. Handle cluster expansion animation. Ensure clustering recalculates on zoom changes. Test performance with 50+ markers to ensure smooth interaction without UI jank.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test clustering behavior at different zoom levels. Verify clusters form when markers are close together and expand when zoomed in. Performance test with 50+ property markers.

Design marker styles: blue pin/icon for rent (operation_type='rent'), green pin/icon for sale (operation_type='sale'). Show price on marker face if space allows (abbreviated: 2.5M, 15K). Use SVG or custom View for marker design matching app aesthetic. Ensure markers are appropriately sized (not too large on map). Consider using tracksViewChanges={false} for performance after initial render. Handle selected state with slight scale increase.

Style callout as mini card with shadow and rounded corners. Ensure callout appears on marker press and dismisses on map press. Handle long property types with ellipsis. Consider thumbnail image if property has video thumbnail_url available.

Request location permission using expo-location: Location.requestForegroundPermissionsAsync(). On permission granted, get current position with Location.getCurrentPositionAsync(). Center map on user location with animateToRegion(). Add user location indicator using showsUserLocation={true} on MapView. If permission denied, default to Guadalajara center (20.6597, -103.3496). Add 'center on me' floating button to re-center on user location. Handle location errors gracefully.

subtasks.title: Implement filter state management with Context/Zustand and WHERE clause builder
subtasks.description: Create centralized filter state management using Context or Zustand, and implement logic to build Supabase query filters from the state.

Define FilterState type with: operationTypes: operation_type[], propertyTypes: property_type[], priceMin: number | null, priceMax: number | null, zone: string | null, bedroomsMin: number | null, petFriendly: boolean, noGuarantor: boolean, studentFriendly: boolean. subtasks.title: Create Edge Function toggle-like with optimistic count update
subtasks.description: Implement supabase/functions/toggle-like/index.ts Edge Function to insert/delete in likes table and atomically update property.like_count
subtasks.details: Create supabase/functions/toggle-like/index.ts: (1) POST endpoint accepting { property_video_id: string, property_id: string }, (2) Get current user from auth.getUser(), return 401 if not authenticated, (3) Check if like exists: SELECT id FROM likes WHERE user_id = current_user AND property_video_id = input, (4) If exists: DELETE from likes, decrement properties.like_count WHERE id = property_id using UPDATE ... SET like_count = like_count - 1, (5) If not exists: INSERT into likes (user_id, property_video_id, property_id), increment like_count + 1, (6) Wrap in transaction for atomicity, (7) Return { liked: boolean, likeCount: number }, (8) Handle edge cases: video not found, property not found, negative count guard (GREATEST(like_count - 1, 0)). Use _shared/ patterns for auth validation and response formatting.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Like creates record and increments count, (2) Unlike deletes record and decrements count, (3) Double-like is idempotent, (4) Unauthenticated request returns 401, (5) Transaction rolls back on partial failure, (6) Count never goes negative

subtasks.title: Create Edge Function toggle-save with optimistic count update
subtasks.description: Implement supabase/functions/toggle-save/index.ts Edge Function to insert/delete in saves table and atomically update property.save_count
subtasks.details: Create supabase/functions/toggle-save/index.ts: (1) POST endpoint accepting { property_id: string }, (2) Get current user from auth.getUser(), return 401 if not authenticated, (3) Check if save exists: SELECT id FROM saves WHERE user_id = current_user AND property_id = input, (4) If exists: DELETE from saves, decrement properties.save_count using UPDATE ... SET save_count = GREATEST(save_count - 1, 0), (5) If not exists: INSERT into saves (user_id, property_id), increment save_count + 1, (6) Wrap in transaction for atomicity, (7) Return { saved: boolean, saveCount: number }, (8) Handle edge cases: property not found (404), negative count guard. Use _shared/ patterns consistent with toggle-like.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Save creates record and increments count, (2) Unsave deletes record and decrements count, (3) Double-save is idempotent, (4) Unauthenticated request returns 401, (5) Transaction atomicity on failure, (6) Count never negative

Create src/lib/api/engagement.ts with typed functions callToggleLike and callToggleSave.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Optimistic update reflects immediately, (2) On success state persists, (3) On network error state reverts, (4) Loading state shows during request, (5) Rapid toggles are debounced, (6) Error toast displays on failure

tasks.title: Create WhatsApp contact with automatic lead generation
tasks.description: Implement contact button that opens WhatsApp with pre-filled message and creates lead record via Edge Function
tasks.details:
1. Create contact button component on property detail and feed overlay
2. Edge Function: supabase/functions/contact-agent/index.ts
3. Input: { propertyId: string }
4. Logic: (a) Get property and agent info, (b) Check if lead already exists (leads table unique on agent_id + user_id), (c) If not exists, insert lead with status='new', (d) Insert lead_origin_properties linking lead to property, (e) Increment property.contact_count, (f) Return agent phone number and pre-filled message
5. Pre-filled WhatsApp message template: 'Hola [Agent Name], vi tu propiedad en Urbea: [Property Address] - [Price]. Me gustaría recibir más información.'
6. Open WhatsApp using Linking.openURL(`whatsapp://send?phone=${phone}&text=${encodeURIComponent(message)}`)
7. Handle case where WhatsApp not installed (show error or fallback to SMS)
8. Idempotent: multiple taps don't create duplicate leads
9. Show toast confirmation: 'Contacto enviado'
10. Test: Contact creates lead, opens WhatsApp, no duplicates
tasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Contact button creates lead in database, (2) WhatsApp opens with correct pre-filled message, (3) Duplicate contacts don't create duplicate leads, (4) Lead shows correct property in lead_origin_properties, (5) property.contact_count increments, (6) Works for both feed and detail screen, (7) Error handling when WhatsApp not installed, (8) Agent phone number is correct

Structure response as { success: boolean, phone: string, message: string } or { error: string }. Follow Edge Function patterns from _shared/ helpers (to be created if needed).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Returns 401 without auth token, (2) Returns 400 with invalid/missing propertyId, (3) Returns 200 with valid input and auth. Verify logs include correlation_id.

subtasks.title: Implement property and agent info retrieval with validation
subtasks.description: Add logic to fetch property details and associated agent information, validating property exists and is active with a valid agent phone number.
subtasks.details: In contact-agent Edge Function: Query properties table joining with users (owner) to get agent info. SELECT p.id, p.address, p.price, p.status, p.owner_user_id, u.display_name as agent_name, u.phone as agent_phone FROM properties p JOIN users u ON p.owner_user_id = u.id WHERE p.id = propertyId AND p.deleted_at IS NULL. Validate: property exists (404 if not), status = 'active' (400 if not contactable), agent has phone number (400 if missing). Return descriptive errors for each validation failure.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Returns 404 for non-existent property, (2) Returns 400 for non-active property status, (3) Returns 400 if agent has no phone, (4) Returns property/agent data for valid active property with agent phone.

subtasks.title: Implement idempotent lead creation with unique constraint check
subtasks.description: Add logic to check if lead exists for agent_id + user_id pair, create new lead if not exists, ensuring idempotency per the unique index leads_agent_user_unique_active.
subtasks.details: Query leads table: SELECT id FROM leads WHERE agent_id = property.owner_user_id AND user_id = auth.uid() AND deleted_at IS NULL. If lead exists, store lead_id for later use (no duplicate creation). If not exists, INSERT INTO leads (agent_id, user_id, status, first_contact_at) VALUES (owner_user_id, auth_user_id, 'new', now()) RETURNING id. Use the existing unique index leads_agent_user_unique_active for idempotency guarantee. Handle race condition by catching unique constraint violation and re-querying existing lead.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) First contact creates new lead with status='new', (2) Second contact on same agent returns existing lead_id without creating duplicate, (3) Concurrent requests don't create duplicates (constraint violation handled gracefully).

subtasks.title: Insert lead_origin_properties and increment property.contact_count
subtasks.description: Link the lead to the originating property via lead_origin_properties table and atomically increment the property's contact_count counter.
subtasks.details: After obtaining lead_id (new or existing): INSERT INTO lead_origin_properties (lead_id, property_id, contacted_at) VALUES (lead_id, propertyId, now()) ON CONFLICT (lead_id, property_id) DO NOTHING. This handles idempotency if user contacts same property multiple times. Then atomically increment counter: UPDATE properties SET contact_count = contact_count + 1 WHERE id = propertyId. Note: per lineamientos §6.4, counters updated in write-time via Edge Functions. Only increment if this is a NEW lead_origin_properties record (use INSERT ... RETURNING or check affected rows).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) New contact creates lead_origin_properties record, (2) Duplicate contact on same property doesn't duplicate lead_origin_properties, (3) contact_count increments only on first contact per property, (4) Multiple properties from same lead each get their own lead_origin_properties entry.

subtasks.title: Generate pre-filled WhatsApp message and return response
subtasks.description: Build the pre-filled WhatsApp message template with property details and return the complete response with agent phone and formatted message.
subtasks.details: Build message template: 'Hola [Agent Name], vi tu propiedad en Urbea: [Property Address] - [Price]. Me gustaría recibir más información.' Format price with Mexican locale (e.g., '$1,500,000 MXN' for sale or '$15,000/mes' for rent). Sanitize agent name and address to prevent injection (remove special chars that could break URL encoding). Return JSON response: { success: true, phone: agent_phone (with country code, e.g., '+52...'), message: prefilledMessage }. Phone should be digits only for WhatsApp URL.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Message contains correct agent name, address, and price, (2) Price formatted correctly for rent vs sale operation types, (3) Phone returned in correct format (digits only with country code), (4) Special characters in address/name sanitized properly.

subtasks.title: Implement WhatsApp deep link opening with fallback and toast confirmation
subtasks.description: Handle WhatsApp opening via Linking.openURL with proper encoding, fallback to SMS if WhatsApp not installed, and show toast confirmation on success.
subtasks.details: In ContactAgentButton component, after receiving Edge Function response: Build URL whatsapp://send?phone=${phone}&text=${encodeURIComponent(message)}. Use Linking.canOpenURL('whatsapp://send') to check if WhatsApp installed. If yes, call Linking.openURL(whatsappUrl). If no, fallback to SMS: Linking.openURL(`sms:${phone}?body=${encodeURIComponent(message)}`). On success, show toast 'Contacto enviado' using react-native-toast-message or similar. Handle Linking.openURL errors gracefully (show error toast). Add analytics event for contact_initiated with propertyId.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) WhatsApp opens with correct pre-filled message when installed, (2) Falls back to SMS when WhatsApp not available, (3) Toast 'Contacto enviado' shows on success, (4) Error toast shows if opening fails, (5) Multiple rapid taps don't trigger multiple Edge Function calls (debounce/loading state).

2. Query: SELECT leads.*, users.full_name, users.profile_photo_url, lead_origin_properties.property_id FROM leads JOIN users ON leads.user_id = users.id LEFT JOIN lead_origin_properties ON leads.id = lead_origin_properties.lead_id WHERE leads.agent_id = current_user AND leads.deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY leads.updated_at DESC
3. Lead card component: User photo/name, lead status badge, property thumbnail, time since last update, tap to expand
4. Expanded view: Status selector dropdown (7 states: new, contacted, qualified, visit_scheduled, visit_done, negotiation, closed), notes textarea, view property button, contact user button (WhatsApp)
5. Edge Function: supabase/functions/update-lead-status/index.ts (validate status transition, update lead.status and lead.updated_at, optionally add note to lead metadata)
6. Filter tabs: All, New, In Progress (contacted-negotiation), Closed
7. Search by user name
8. Empty state for new agents
9. Test: Lead status updates, list filters work, property link works
tasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Agent sees only their leads, (2) Lead list displays correct info, (3) Status update persists to database, (4) Status badge colors match status, (5) Filter tabs work correctly, (6) Property link navigates to correct property, (7) WhatsApp contact button works, (8) Search filters leads, (9) Empty state shows for agents with no leads, (10) Owner can see their agents' leads (if agency_id matches)

RLS already filters by agent_id. Include pagination with limit/offset or cursor for scalability.

subtasks.title: Create Edge Function update-lead-status with validation
subtasks.description: Create supabase/functions/update-lead-status/index.ts Edge Function to validate status transitions, update lead.status and lead.updated_at, and optionally add note to internal_notes.
subtasks.details:
Create supabase/functions/update-lead-status/index.ts. Accepts POST with body: {lead_id, new_status, note?}. Validate: (1) user is authenticated, (2) user is agent_id or admin (reuse existing auth pattern from other functions), (3) new_status is valid enum value, (4) optional: validate transition rules if any (e.g., cannot go from closed_won back to new - decide if needed for MVP). Update: UPDATE leads SET status = $new_status, updated_at = now(), internal_notes = CASE WHEN $note THEN internal_notes || '
[' || to_char(now(), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI') || '] ' || $note ELSE internal_notes END WHERE id = $lead_id. Return updated lead. Use _shared/ patterns for auth, validation, CORS, error handling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Only agent_id of lead or admin can update, (2) Invalid status returns 400, (3) Status updates correctly, (4) updated_at is set to now(), (5) Note appends to internal_notes with timestamp, (6) Returns updated lead object

subtasks.description: Implement search input to filter leads by user name (full_name). Search should be case-insensitive and filter the current list or modify the query.
subtasks.details: Add TextInput with search icon at top of CRM screen (above or alongside filter tabs). State: searchQuery string. Two approaches: (1) Client-side filter on fetched data using leads.filter(l => l.users.full_name.toLowerCase().includes(query)), good for small lists. (2) Server-side: add ILIKE '%{query}%' to query. Recommend client-side for MVP simplicity unless list is very large. Debounce search input (300ms) to avoid excessive updates. Clear button to reset search. Search combines with active filter tab.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Typing filters leads by user name, (2) Search is case-insensitive, (3) Empty search shows all leads, (4) Search combines with filter tabs, (5) Clear button resets search, (6) Debounce prevents excessive filtering

subtasks.description: Set up app/profile/[id].tsx for viewing any agent's profile and app/(tabs)/profile.tsx for the current user's own profile tab, with proper Expo Router configuration and navigation between screens
subtasks.details:
Create both profile screens with Expo Router dynamic routing. app/profile/[id].tsx receives the agent ID as a route parameter via useLocalSearchParams(). app/(tabs)/profile.tsx uses the authenticated user's ID from AuthContext. Both screens share a common ProfileView component but with different data sources and conditional UI (edit/logout buttons only on own profile). subtasks.title: Create Edge Function update-property-status for status transitions
subtasks.description: Build Edge Function to validate and execute property status transitions, handling closed_reason for closing actions
subtasks.details: Create supabase/functions/update-property-status/index.ts. Accept property_id, new_status, and optional closed_reason in request body. Validate user owns the property (owner_user_id = auth.uid()). Validate status transitions: draft->active (publish), active->paused, paused->active, active/paused->closed (requires closed_reason from enum: rented, sold, withdrawn, expired). Update properties table with new status, closed_reason if closing. Return updated property. Handle errors: not found, unauthorized, invalid transition, missing closed_reason.

6. Create 1-2 buscador (seeker) users for demo interactions
7. Document test passwords in code comments for dev team
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) ~15 agent users created, (2) Each agency has 1 owner + 2-3 agents, (3) All agents have role='agent' and is_verified_agent=true, (4) agency_members records link users to agencies correctly, (5) Test login works with demo+agent1@urbea.mx

subtasks.title: Create 10-20 properties with varied types, zones, and price ranges
subtasks.description: Insert 10-20 properties distributed across agencies with variety: operation_type (rent/sale), property_type (casa/departamento/etc), zones (Providencia, Chapultepec, Americana, Zapopan, Tlaquepaque), realistic price ranges (5k-50k rent, 2M-15M sale), bedrooms (1-4), and authentic Guadalajara addresses.
subtasks.details:
1. Define 10-20 property UUIDs using pattern 30000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000XX
2. Distribute properties across agencies (roughly even distribution)
3. Create variety:
   - Operation: ~50% rent, ~50% sale
   - Type: mix of casa, departamento, terreno, local
   - Zones: Providencia, Chapultepec, Americana, Zapopan Centro, Tlaquepaque Centro
   - Rent prices: 5,000-50,000 MXN
   - Sale prices: 2,000,000-15,000,000 MXN
   - Bedrooms: 1-4, Bathrooms: 1-3
4. Use realistic PostGIS coordinates for each zone
5. Set status='active', published_at=now() for all demo properties
6. Write descriptive text for each property in Spanish
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) 10-20 properties created, (2) Mix of rent/sale operations, (3) Properties distributed across 5 zones, (4) Price ranges are realistic, (5) All properties have valid PostGIS locations, (6) All properties status='active'

subtasks.title: Source or create 3-5 sample vertical real estate videos
subtasks.description: Find or create 3-5 royalty-free vertical (9:16) real estate videos suitable for the demo. Videos should be short (15-60 seconds), show property interiors/exteriors, and be legally clear for demo use.
subtasks.details:
1. Search royalty-free video sources: Pexels, Pixabay, Coverr for vertical real estate content
2. Criteria: vertical format (9:16), 15-60 seconds, real estate content (interiors, exteriors, tours)
3. Download 3-5 videos that show variety (apartments, houses, different styles)
4. Verify license allows commercial use and demo purposes
5. Store videos temporarily in a known location (e.g., /tmp/demo-videos/ or supabase/demo-assets/)
6. Document video sources and licenses in a comment or README
7. If no suitable royalty-free videos found, document alternatives (create simple screen recordings or use placeholder)
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) 3-5 videos obtained, (2) All videos are vertical format, (3) Licenses are documented, (4) Videos are accessible locally for upload script

subtasks.title: Create script to upload videos to Storage and insert property_videos records
subtasks.description: Write a script (shell or Node.js) that uploads sample videos to the Supabase Storage bucket and inserts corresponding property_videos records with storage_path and status='ready'. Requires Task 4 (storage_path column) to be completed first.
subtasks.details:
1. Create upload script: supabase/scripts/seed-videos.sh or .ts
2. Configure Storage bucket name (e.g., 'property-videos')
3. For each video file:
   a. Upload to Storage using supabase CLI or SDK
   b. Get the storage_path (e.g., 'demo/video-1.mp4')
4. Map videos to properties (distribute across 10-20 properties, 1-2 videos each)
5. Insert property_videos records:
   - property_id, storage_path, status='ready', position=1
   - duration_seconds (from video metadata or estimate)
   - ready_at=now()
6. Ensure script is idempotent (can be re-run without duplicates)
7. Add script to seed.sql execution flow or document as separate step
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Script runs without errors, (2) Videos uploaded to Storage bucket, (3) property_videos records created with storage_path, (4) All videos have status='ready', (5) Script is idempotent

subtasks.title: Create demo likes, saves, and leads between users for CRM testing
subtasks.description: Insert realistic interactions between demo users: likes on videos, saves on properties, and leads in the CRM. This provides data for testing the feed engagement features and CRM functionality.
subtasks.details:
1. Create likes:
   - Buscador users like various property_videos
   - Insert into likes: user_id, property_video_id, property_id, created_at
   - Update properties.like_count (denormalized counter)
2. Create saves:
   - Buscador users save properties
   - Insert into saves: user_id, property_id
   - Update properties.save_count
3. Create leads:

4. Update properties.contact_count for properties with leads
5. Ensure unique constraints are respected (no duplicate likes/saves/leads)
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Likes created for various videos, (2) Saves created for properties, (3) 5-10 leads created with varied statuses, (4) lead_origin_properties links leads to properties, (5) Denormalized counters updated correctly

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down seeding into: (1) extend seed.sql with 3-5 Guadalajara agencies, (2) create owners and agents with test accounts, (3) create 10-20 properties with varied types and locations, (4) source 3-5 royalty-free vertical real estate videos, (5) create script to upload videos and insert property_videos records, (6) create demo likes, saves, and leads for CRM testing, (7) document reset process and verify seed data loads correctly

tasks.title: Design branding and component system (pending client approval)
tasks.description: Create Urbea brand identity (logo, color palette, typography) and design system using CODE-FIRST approach (theme.ts + component library in preview), deferring Figma to Full/Dev seat availability
tasks.details:
⚠️ GATE: Per PRD section 8 and CLAUDE.md §8, do NOT start implementation until client gives express approval.

REORIENTATION (exploration 001-branding-design-system-urbea, approved 2026-06-21): CODE-FIRST route instead of Figma-first. Figma seat is View/Starter (~6 MCP calls/month), insufficient for iteration; Figma DEFERRED until Full/Dev seat.

DESIGN DECISIONS (derived from URBEARG sibling project):
- Direction A: dual-mode tokens with SINGLE brand-core (light=management, dark=feed) → one theme.ts, never hardcoded colors
- Canonical palette = URBEARG SHIPPED: primary #1F3B2D, accent #B08968, sand #EEE5DA, ink #1A1A1A
- Dark feed derivation (WCAG AA to verify): bg #0F1A11, text #F7F3EC, accent lightened #C79A77
- Typography: Fraunces (premium serif, optical sizing) for display/titles + Inter for body/UI
- Logo: 2-3 concepts to approve direction; final artwork later

PHASES (replaces Figma-centric plan):
0. Gate: client approval
1. Identity/palette: logo concepts, canonical palette confirmation, typography selection
2. Tokens: theme.ts (light/dark), spacing/typography/radius/shadow scales
3. Base components: Button/Input/Card/Badge/BottomSheet in HTML/React preview (NOT Figma)
4. Key screens: Feed (dark), Detail (dark→light), Wizard (light), CRM (light)
5. Figma DEFERRED to seat upgrade

BRANDING GATE REMAINS ACTIVE (CLAUDE.md §8): no implementation without client approval. Doc: .taskmaster/docs/exploraciones/001-branding-design-system-urbea.md
tasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Client approved brand direction and canonical palette (GATE blocker), (2) Direction A (dual-mode tokens with single brand-core) confirmed, (3) WCAG 2.1 AA contrast verified on critical feed pairs (text on dark bg, accent on dark), (4) theme.ts (prospective) with semantic snake_case tokens for light and dark, (5) Base component library (Button/Input/Card/Badge/BottomSheet) consumes tokens and renders in preview, (6) 4 key screens mocked up with component library, (7) Feed contrasts (dark/immersive) vs management (light/clean) cohesively, (8) Figma scope deferred and reflected in plan, (9) pnpm tsc --noEmit and pnpm lint clean on theme/component code (when implemented)

subtasks.title: Gate: Obtain client approval for branding direction and canonical palette
subtasks.description: Present Direction A (dual-mode tokens with single URBEARG-derived brand-core), canonical palette (shipped URBEARG values), dark feed derivation, Fraunces+Inter typography, and 2-3 logo concepts. Secure explicit client approval before proceeding to implementation.
subtasks.details: BLOCKING GATE per CLAUDE.md §8 and PRD §8. Present to client: (1) Direction A rationale - dual-mode tokens (light management, dark feed) with single brand-core from URBEARG; (2) Canonical palette - SHIPPED URBEARG values (primary #1F3B2D, accent #B08968, sand #EEE5DA, ink #1A1A1A), reconciliation note (shipped wins over spec); (3) Dark feed derivation - bg #0F1A11, text #F7F3EC, accent #C79A77 (WCAG AA to verify); (4) Typography - Fraunces (serif, optical sizing) for display, Inter for body/UI; (5) 2-3 logo direction concepts (wordmark/symbol/combination); (6) Code-first approach with Figma deferred. Document approval decision and any client modifications. Do NOT proceed to subtask 2 without explicit approval.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Client approval documented (date, decision), (2) Direction confirmed (A or alternate), (3) Palette confirmed (canonical or modified), (4) Typography approved (Fraunces+Inter or alternate), (5) Logo direction selected, (6) Code-first approach approved.

subtasks.title: Define and document canonical Urbea palette with WCAG AA verification
subtasks.description: Finalize canonical URBEARG-derived palette (light mode ~1:1) and dark feed derivation, verify critical contrast pairs against WCAG 2.1 AA, document in exploration 001.
subtasks.details: Brand-core (source): primary #1F3B2D (hover #0F1A11), accent #B08968 (hover #9A7356), sand #EEE5DA, ink #1A1A1A. Light mode tokens (management screens): color_bg=#EEE5DA, color_surface=#FFFFFF, color_text=#1A1A1A, color_text_muted=#78716C, color_border=#E7E5E4, color_primary=#1F3B2D, color_accent=#B08968. Dark mode tokens (feed): color_bg=#0F1A11, color_surface=rgba(15,26,17,0.72)+blur, color_text=#F7F3EC, color_text_muted=#C9C1B5, color_accent=#C79A77 (lightened for contrast), color_primary_on_dark=#3E6B50, color_overlay_scrim=gradient. CRITICAL: Use contrast checker tool to verify WCAG 2.1 AA (4.5:1 normal text, 3:1 large text) on: (a) #F7F3EC on #0F1A11 (feed text on bg), (b) #C79A77 on #0F1A11 (accent on dark bg), (c) #1A1A1A on #EEE5DA (light mode text). If fails, adjust lightness. Document verified values in 001-branding-design-system-urbea.md. Deliverable: palette table with verified contrast ratios.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) All token pairs documented (light and dark), (2) Critical feed pairs meet WCAG 2.1 AA (text=#F7F3EC, accent=#C79A77 on bg=#0F1A11), (3) Light mode pairs meet AA, (4) Contrast ratios documented, (5) If any fail, values adjusted and re-verified.

subtasks.title: Finalize typography system: Fraunces (display) + Inter (body/UI)
subtasks.description: Confirm Fraunces variable font (optical sizing for mobile) for display/titles and Inter for body/UI. Define type scale (display, heading h1-h4, body, caption, label) with sizes, line heights, weights.
subtasks.details: Typography decision (approved): Fraunces (serif, premium, optical sizing via opsz axis) for display/hero titles only (NOT dense UI, where serif hurts legibility on mobile); Inter (sans, workhorse) for body/paragraphs/UI. Both available via Google Fonts and loadable in RN/Expo via expo-font/@expo-google-fonts. Fraunces is variable → leverage opsz (optical size) and wght (weight) axes; potential SOFT/WONK for personality in large titles. Type scale to define: (1) Display - Fraunces opsz=72 wght=600-700, size ~32-40px, line-height 1.1-1.2, usage: hero titles (property detail, feed overlay price); (2) Heading h1-h4 - Fraunces opsz=18-24 wght=500-600, sizes 24/20/18/16px, line-height 1.3, usage: section headers; (3) Body - Inter wght=400-500, size 16/14px, line-height 1.5, usage: paragraphs, descriptions; (4) Caption - Inter wght=400, size 12px, line-height 1.4, usage: metadata, timestamps; (5) Label - Inter wght=500-600, size 14/12px, usage: buttons, form labels. Specify font file loading strategy (expo-font loadAsync or expo-google-fonts package). Document in 001 and prepare for theme.ts implementation. Deliverable: type scale table with font family, size, line-height, weight, usage per level.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Fraunces and Inter confirmed and available via Expo, (2) Type scale covers all UI needs (display to caption), (3) Fraunces usage limited to display/titles (not dense UI), (4) Sizes are mobile-appropriate (16px+ for body, 12px min for caption), (5) Optical sizing strategy documented (opsz axis values), (6) Scale ready for theme.ts export.

subtasks.title: Create prospective theme.ts with dual-mode semantic tokens
subtasks.description: Implement theme.ts file with light and dark mode tokens (colors, spacing scale, typography, radii, shadows) in snake_case. Export typed theme object and useTheme hook for mode switching by surface (feed=dark, management=light).
subtasks.details: DEPENDENCY: mobile/ does NOT exist yet (task #1 pending); this is PROSPECTIVE code to prepare structure. Create src/theme/theme.ts (or document structure for later implementation): (1) Colors - nested objects light: { color_bg, color_surface, color_text, color_text_muted, color_border, color_primary, color_accent, color_ring, ... semantic colors (success, warning, error) } and dark: { color_bg, color_surface, color_text, color_text_muted, color_accent, color_primary_on_dark, color_overlay_scrim }. Use verified palette values from subtask 2. (2) Spacing scale - 8px base grid (URBEARG): space_xs=4, space_sm=8, space_md=12, space_base=16, space_lg=20, space_xl=24, space_2xl=32, space_3xl=40, space_4xl=48, space_5xl=64. (3) Typography - font_family_display='Fraunces', font_family_body='Inter', font_size/line_height/weight scales per subtask 3. (4) Border radius - radius_none=0, radius_sm=4, radius_md=8, radius_lg=12, radius_full=9999. (5) Shadows (RN shadow objects for iOS, elevation for Android) - shadow_sm, shadow_md, shadow_lg. (6) Export typed const theme = { light, dark, spacing, typography, radius, shadows } and ThemeProvider context + useTheme() hook that returns current mode tokens. Mode logic: feed routes (e.g., app/(tabs)/index.tsx, property detail video section) = dark; other routes (wizard, CRM, profile, admin) = light. TypeScript strict typing for all values. Follow snake_case for tokens (urbea-design skill), PascalCase for components. Document in code comments: PROSPECTIVE - will be implemented after task #1 (Expo init). Deliverable: theme.ts structure document or skeleton file.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) theme.ts structure defined with light/dark/spacing/typography/radius/shadows, (2) All tokens use snake_case, (3) Verified palette values from subtask 2 used, (4) Type scale from subtask 3 integrated, (5) TypeScript strict types defined (ThemeMode, ThemeColors, etc.), (6) useTheme hook and ThemeProvider logic documented, (7) Mode-by-surface logic specified (feed=dark, management=light), (8) Code compiles with pnpm tsc --noEmit (when mobile/ exists).

subtasks.title: Design and implement Button component with variants in preview
subtasks.description: Create Button component consuming theme tokens with variants (primary, accent, ghost, destructive), sizes (sm, md, lg), and states (default, pressed, disabled, loading). Validate in HTML/React preview (NOT Expo yet).
subtasks.details: PROSPECTIVE component (mobile/ does not exist; task #1 pending). Structure src/components/Button.tsx: (1) Props interface - variant: 'primary'|'accent'|'ghost'|'destructive', size: 'sm'|'md'|'lg', disabled: boolean, loading: boolean, onPress, children, accessibilityLabel; (2) Variant styles - primary: bg=color_primary, text=color_surface (light mode) or color_text (dark); accent: bg=color_accent; ghost: transparent bg, border=color_border, text=color_text; destructive: bg=#DC2626 (semantic red). (3) Size styles - sm: height=32px, px=space_md, font_size=14px; md: height=40px, px=space_lg, font_size=16px; lg: height=48px, px=space_xl, font_size=18px. (4) States - pressed: opacity 0.8 or bg darken 10%; disabled: opacity 0.4, pointer-events none; loading: spinner + text hidden or "Cargando...". (5) Consume tokens via useTheme() hook; NO hardcoded colors. (6) Accessibility - accessibilityRole="button", accessibilityState={{ disabled }}, accessibilityLabel. Preview strategy: since Expo app doesn't exist, create a standalone React preview page (HTML/React or Storybook-like) OR document component structure with mock theme values. Use ui-ux-pro-max or building-native-ui skills if helpful. TypeScript strict. Deliverable: Button component code or detailed spec + preview screenshot/demo.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Button component defined with all variants/sizes/states, (2) Consumes theme tokens via useTheme (no hardcoded colors), (3) TypeScript props interface strict, (4) Accessibility props included, (5) Preview renders button in light and dark modes (or mock documented), (6) Pressed/disabled/loading states visually distinguishable, (7) Code compiles (pnpm tsc --noEmit when mobile/ exists).

subtasks.title: Design and implement Input component with states in preview
subtasks.description: Create Input (text field) component with label, helper text, error state, and icon support. Validate in HTML/React preview.
subtasks.details: PROSPECTIVE component. Structure src/components/Input.tsx: (1) Props - value, onChangeText, placeholder, label (optional string), helperText (optional), error (boolean or string), disabled, icon (optional component), secureTextEntry (for password), accessibilityLabel; (2) Layout - label above input (font_size caption, color_text_muted), input field (height 40-48px, border=color_border 1px, radius_md, px=space_md, bg=color_surface, color_text), helperText or error below (font_size caption, color_text_muted or red if error); (3) States - default: border=color_border; focused: border=color_primary (or color_ring), outline/ring effect; error: border=red, helperText red; disabled: opacity 0.6, bg=color_surface_muted. (4) Icon - if provided, render left or right of input (adjust px). (5) Consume theme tokens via useTheme. (6) Accessibility - accessibilityLabel, accessibilityHint for helperText, accessibilityState={{ disabled }}. Preview in standalone React page or documented structure. TypeScript strict. Deliverable: Input component code or spec + preview.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Input component defined with label/helperText/error/icon, (2) Consumes theme tokens (no hardcoded colors), (3) Focused/error/disabled states visually clear, (4) TypeScript props strict, (5) Accessibility props included, (6) Preview renders input in light and dark modes, (7) Code compiles (when mobile/ exists).

subtasks.title: Design and implement Card, Badge, and BottomSheet components in preview
subtasks.description: Create Card (base + PropertyCard/AgentCard/LeadCard variants), Badge (status indicators), and BottomSheet (modal sheet) components. Validate in preview.
subtasks.details: PROSPECTIVE components. (1) Card.tsx - base Card with children, bg=color_surface, radius_lg, shadow_md, px/py=space_lg. Variants: PropertyCard (thumbnail, price, location, specs layout), AgentCard (avatar, name, agency, contact button), LeadCard (lead name, status badge, property origin, timestamp). (2) Badge.tsx - small colored chip for status. Props: variant (status badges: draft/active/paused/closed, lead statuses: nuevo/contactado/visita_agendada/visita_realizada/negociacion/cerrado_ganado/cerrado_perdido). Each variant maps to color (from theme semantics or custom per PRD lead states). Size sm (height 24px, px=space_sm, font_size 12px), rounded radius_full or radius_md. (3) BottomSheet.tsx - modal overlay from bottom. Props: visible, onClose, children. Layout: overlay scrim (color_overlay_scrim or rgba(0,0,0,0.5)), sheet container (bg=color_surface, radius_lg top corners, shadow_lg, max-height 80%, handle indicator at top). All consume theme tokens via useTheme. Accessibility: Card accessibilityRole="button" if tappable, Badge accessibilityLabel with status text, BottomSheet modal accessibility (trap focus, announce to screen readers). Preview in React page or document structure. TypeScript strict. Deliverable: Card/Badge/BottomSheet code or spec + preview.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Card, Badge, BottomSheet components defined, (2) Card variants (PropertyCard/AgentCard/LeadCard) structured, (3) Badge variants map to status colors (7 lead states + publication states), (4) BottomSheet has overlay scrim and handle, (5) All consume theme tokens, (6) TypeScript props strict, (7) Accessibility props included, (8) Preview renders in light/dark modes, (9) Code compiles.

subtasks.title: Mock up Feed screen (dark/immersive) with component library
subtasks.description: Design Feed screen mockup using dark mode tokens and base components. Show full-screen video placeholder, overlay gradient, property info Card, agent info, like/save/contact Buttons. Validate in preview.
subtasks.details: PROSPECTIVE screen mockup (mobile/ does not exist; task #1 and #9 will implement). Create Feed.mockup (HTML/React preview or Figma-less design doc): (1) Layout - full-screen vertical (simulate mobile viewport 375x812 or 390x844), dark mode bg=color_bg (#0F1A11); (2) Video placeholder - full-screen rectangle (simulate expo-video Video component), placeholder bg=dark gray or video thumbnail; (3) Overlay gradient - linear gradient from transparent (top 60%) to rgba(0,0,0,0.6) (bottom 40%) to ensure text legibility (color_overlay_scrim from theme); (4) Property info - bottom 1/3, Card-like layout (but overlay, not separate surface): price (Display typography, Fraunces, color_text=#F7F3EC), property type + location (Body typography, Inter, color_text_muted), specs (bedrooms, bathrooms, sqm) as inline chips or text; (5) Agent info - small AgentCard or inline (avatar circle, agent name, agency name, Button "Contactar" variant=accent); (6) Actions - heart icon (like), bookmark icon (save), share icon (top-right or side); (7) Swipe indicators - small dots or progress bar on side to show position in feed. subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Feed mockup uses dark mode tokens exclusively (bg=#0F1A11, text=#F7F3EC, accent=#C79A77), (2) Overlay gradient ensures text legibility over video placeholder, (3) Property price uses Fraunces display, (4) Agent info and CTAs use Button/Card components, (5) Layout is mobile-optimized (thumb zones, safe areas), (6) Preview visually matches immersive/premium aesthetic, (7) Typography scale and spacing scale applied correctly.

subtasks.title: Mock up Property Detail, Wizard, and CRM screens (light mode) with component library
subtasks.description: Design Property Detail (dark→light transition), Publication Wizard (3 steps, clean/light), and CRM (utilitarian/light) mockups using light mode tokens and base components. Validate in preview.
subtasks.details: PROSPECTIVE screen mockups. (1) Property Detail - hero section (video or gallery, dark bg similar to Feed for continuity), then transition to light mode (bg=color_bg sand) for details section: price/specs, description (Body typography, Inter), map preview, agent contact Card, similar properties list. Use Card for property specs, Button for contact CTA. (2) Publication Wizard - 3 steps (per task #8 PRD): step indicator (1/3, 2/3, 3/3) at top, light bg=color_bg, clean/minimal. Step 1: operation_type and property_type selection (large tappable Cards or Buttons). Step 2: form with Input components (price, bedrooms, bathrooms, sqm, address autocomplete, description textarea), map picker placeholder, checkboxes (pet_friendly, etc.) as toggle Buttons or Badges. All screens consume light theme tokens (bg=sand, surface=white, text=ink, border, primary green). Typography Inter for body/labels, Fraunces for section headers. Document that these routes trigger ThemeProvider mode='light'. Deliverable: 3 screen mockups (Property Detail, Wizard, CRM) in preview or design spec.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Property Detail has dark hero → light details transition, (2) Wizard uses light mode tokens, 3-step layout clear, form uses Input/Button components, (3) CRM uses light mode, LeadCard with 7-state Badges, utilitarian/clean aesthetic, (4) All screens consume light theme tokens (bg=sand, surface=white, text=ink), (5) Typography scale applied (Inter body, Fraunces headers where appropriate), (6) Spacing scale consistent, (7) Preview visually contrasts with Feed (light vs dark), (8) Layout is mobile-optimized.

subtasks.title: Document Figma deferral and code-first workflow in vault and task plan
subtasks.description: Update exploration 001, wiki/codebase/mapa-codebase.md, and task #19 notes to reflect code-first approach, Figma deferred to Full/Dev seat, and canonical palette/typography decisions. Prepare for vault ingest when subtasks complete.
subtasks.details: Documentation and vault maintenance (per CLAUDE.md §7 Karpathy pattern): (1) Update .taskmaster/docs/exploraciones/001-branding-design-system-urbea.md - mark estado=aprobado (when gate passed), document final palette values with verified contrast ratios, typography selection, logo direction chosen, code-first workflow confirmed, Figma scope deferred. Link to relevant concepts: [[urbea-design]] (design system skill), exploration 001. tasks.title: Create Edge Function to mint signed video URLs with service_role
tasks.description: Build Edge Function (get-feed or mint-video-url) that uses service_role client to generate signed URLs for property videos, bypassing RLS SELECT policy to support 2-segment storage paths ({user_id}/{video_id}.mp4) in the feed.
tasks.details:
**CONTEXT:** Task #8 (publish wizard) uploads videos with 2-segment path `{user_id}/{video_id}.mp4` (Option C). The RLS SELECT policy `property_videos_storage_select` (migration 20260604000011:57) grants public read via `private.property_is_public((foldername)[2]::uuid)`, which assumes property_id in the 2nd segment. With 2-segment paths, `foldername[2] = '{video_id}.mp4'` (::uuid cast fails) → non-owner users cannot read the video (only owner via `foldername[1]=auth.uid()`).

**SOLUTION (orchestrator+user):** DO NOT modify RLS or path structure. The feed obtains playback URLs via an Edge Function (e.g., `get-feed` or `mint-video-url`) that uses `service_role` client and calls `createSignedUrl(storage_path)` → bypasses anon RLS SELECT, 2-segment path irrelevant. Same pattern we'll use when migrating to Cloudflare R2 in production (server-side presigned URLs).

**ALTERNATIVES DISCARDED:** (B) SECURITY DEFINER helper `video_storage_is_public(name)` by storage_path; (C) re-work to 3-segment paths.

**IMPLEMENTATION:**

1. **Create Edge Function `supabase/functions/get-feed/`** (or `mint-video-url/` if separate):
   - Follow DI pattern (task #5): `handler.ts` (pure logic + injectable deps) + `index.ts` (prod entry with `Deno.serve`) + `handler.test.ts`.
   - Entry: `index.ts` imports `service_client()` from `_shared/clients.ts`, constructs deps, calls `handler(req, deps)`.

2. **Handler logic (`handler.ts`):**
   - Method: GET or POST (decide based on feed needs; likely POST with array of property_ids or GET with query params).
   - Input validation: Accept `property_id` or array of `property_id`s.
   - Query `public.properties` + `public.property_videos` JOIN to fetch `storage_path` for active/ready videos:
     ```sql
     SELECT pv.id, pv.storage_path 
     FROM property_videos pv
     INNER JOIN properties p ON p.id = pv.property_id
     WHERE p.id = ANY($1) AND p.status = 'active' AND pv.status = 'ready' AND pv.deleted_at IS NULL
     ```
   - For each `storage_path`, call `supabase.storage.from('property-videos').createSignedUrl(storage_path, expiresIn)`:
     - `expiresIn`: 3600 seconds (1 hour) for feed playback.
     - Returns `{ signedUrl, error }`.
   - Return map: `{ [video_id]: signed_url }` or array `[{ video_id, signed_url, property_id }]`.

3. **Dependencies interface (`types.ts` or inline in `handler.ts`):**
   - `VideoUrlMinter`: interface with `mint_signed_urls(property_ids: string[]) => Promise<MintResult>`.
   - Real adapter in `_shared/clients.ts`: `make_video_url_minter(client: SupabaseClient)` → queries DB + calls `createSignedUrl` with service_role (bypasses RLS).

4. **Error handling:**
   - 400 INVALID_INPUT: missing/invalid property_ids.
   - 404 NOT_FOUND: no videos found for given property_ids.
   - 500 INTERNAL_ERROR: storage API failure.

5. **Tests (`handler.test.ts`):**
   - Mock `VideoUrlMinter` dependency.
   - Test: valid property_id → returns signed URL.
   - Test: array of property_ids → returns map/array of signed URLs.
   - Test: property not found → 404.
   - Test: video not ready → excluded from results.
   - Test: storage_path null → excluded (shouldn't happen if query is correct).

6. **Integration with feed (task #9 dependency):**
   - Feed query fetches properties, then calls this Edge Function with batch of property_ids.
   - Client receives signed URLs, uses them in video player (expo-av or similar).
   - Signed URLs expire in 1 hour; feed must refresh URLs if user stays longer (or implement token refresh pattern).

7. **Update `_shared/clients.ts`:**
   - Add `make_video_url_minter(client: SupabaseClient): VideoUrlMinter` adapter.
   - Use `client.storage.from('property-videos').createSignedUrl(path, expiresIn)`.
   - Map errors to structured result.

8. **Migration/DB considerations:**
   - NO changes to RLS policies or storage paths.
   - Confirm migration 20260604000011 RLS SELECT policy remains unchanged.
   - Confirm migration 20260604000012 CHECK constraint (`ready` requires `storage_path` OR `cloudflare_uid`) is satisfied.

9. **Security audit:**
   - service_role bypasses RLS → MUST verify property.status='active' in SQL query (don't mint URLs for draft/paused/closed properties).
   - Signed URLs are time-limited (1 hour) → acceptable for feed use case.
   - No auth required for this endpoint (public feed) OR require anon/authenticated JWT but don't rely on RLS (decide based on PRD §9 feed visibility).

10. **Deploy and verify:**
    - Deploy Edge Function: `supabase functions deploy get-feed` (or `mint-video-url`).
    - Test with curl: `curl -X POST https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/functions/v1/get-feed -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"property_ids":["<uuid>"]}'`.
    - Verify signed URL works: fetch signed URL in browser or with `curl` → should return video file.

**FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY:**
- `supabase/functions/get-feed/handler.ts` (or `mint-video-url/handler.ts`)
- `supabase/functions/get-feed/index.ts`
- `supabase/functions/get-feed/types.ts`
- `supabase/functions/get-feed/handler.test.ts`
- `supabase/functions/_shared/clients.ts` (add `make_video_url_minter`)
- Update `wiki/codebase/mapa-codebase.md` after implementation (add entry for video URL minting → [[propiedades-y-video]] or [[feed-vertical-video]]).
tasks.testStrategy:
**VERIFICATION STEPS:**

1. **Unit tests pass (`handler.test.ts`):**
   - Run `deno test` from `supabase/functions/` directory.
   - Verify all test cases pass: valid input → signed URLs, invalid input → 400, not found → 404, batch minting works.

2. **Edge Function deploys successfully:**
   - `supabase functions deploy get-feed` (or `mint-video-url`) completes without errors.
   - Function appears in Supabase Dashboard → Edge Functions.

3. **Manual curl test (POST with property_id array):**
   - Seed a test property with status='active' and property_video with status='ready' and storage_path='{user_id}/{video_id}.mp4'.
   - `curl -X POST https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/functions/v1/get-feed -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"property_ids":["<property-uuid>"]}'`.
   - Response: 200 OK with JSON `{ "videos": [{ "video_id": "<uuid>", "signed_url": "https://..." }] }` (or similar structure).

4. **Signed URL playback verification:**
   - Copy signed URL from response.
   - Open in browser or `curl` the signed URL → should return video/mp4 content (not 403/404).
   - Verify URL includes query params `?token=...&exp=...`.

5. **RLS bypass confirmation (non-owner can access video):**
   - Create property owned by user A (user_id in storage_path = A's UUID).
   - Call Edge Function as anon or user B (not owner).
   - Verify signed URL is returned and video is accessible → confirms service_role bypasses RLS SELECT policy.

6. **Only active properties minted:**
   - Seed property with status='draft' or 'paused'.
   - Call Edge Function with that property_id.
   - Response: 404 or empty array (no signed URL for non-active properties).

7. **Only ready videos minted:**
   - Seed property_video with status='processing' or 'failed'.
   - Call Edge Function.
   - Response: no signed URL for non-ready videos.

8. **Expiration works:**
   - Mint signed URL with short expiration (e.g., 60 seconds for test).
   - Wait until expiration.
   - Attempt to fetch video via signed URL → should fail (403 or expired token error).

9. **Integration with feed (task #9 dependency check):**
   - Mock or stub this Edge Function in feed implementation.
   - Feed fetches properties, calls get-feed with property_ids batch, receives signed URLs, passes to video player.
   - Confirm feed can autoplay videos using signed URLs.

10. **Vault ingestion:**
    - Update `wiki/codebase/mapa-codebase.md`: add row for get-feed Edge Function → [[feed-vertical-video]] or [[propiedades-y-video]].
    - Add entry to `wiki/log.md`: `## [YYYY-MM-DD] feat | Edge Function get-feed para mintar signed URLs de video con service_role`.
